english: frq.; nam ₒgró-na (nam 'gro na) when I (you etc.) go, was going, shall go. — b. if, in case, sup-posing that (ἐάν), the different degrees of possibility, however, cannot be so precisely expressed by the mood in Tibetan, as in other languages; with or without a preced-ing gál-te (gal te), ći-ste (ci ste) etc. (cf. the remarks sub gaṅ (gang) II.); ... ma mtóṅ na (ma mthong na) ... mi rtógs-par dug (mi rtogs par 'dug) if we had not seen ..., we should not have known ... Mil.; but in most cases also the vb., to which it is subordinate, is put in the gerund: ₒdi byás-na brám-ze ma yin-pas ('di byas na bram ze ma yin pas) as I should be no longer a Brahmin, if I were to do that Dzl.; further: if even ..., how much the more ...! in asseverations: if ..., then indeed may ...! then I would that ...! it is well, that ..., it will be well, if ..., na légs-so (na legs so) frq.; if légs-so (legs so) is elliptically omitted, na (na) answers to: o that! would that! also: I will; in an interrogative sen-tence, viz. 'légs-sam' ('legs sam') being omitted, to: must I? shall I? Mil.: ćos byás-na snyam (chos byas na snyam) (when we are with you) we think, we will be pious! ₒjig-rtén byás-na snyam ('jig rten byas na snyam) (when we have come home) we think, let us take care of temporal things! ći drág-na (ci drag na) (better ći byás-na drag (ci byas na drag)) what shall we consider the most advantageous? — c. of a more general signification: as, since, whilst, by (with the partic. pres.), = te (te) or pas (pas) Dzl. frq., dug zós-na yaṅ (dug zos na yang) even by eating poi-sonous things (he was not hurt) 2, 3; na (na) is used thus. however, only in con-junction with yaṅ (yang), and dug zós-na yaṅ (dug zos na yang) is the more popular phrase for dug zos kyaṅ (dug zos kyang) In careless speaking or writing na (na) is also used for će-na (ce na) Thgy. frq. — 3. pleon. added to the termination of the instr. of substan-tives and verbs: rgyu dés-na (rgyu des na) for that reason, therefore, ćiï rgyús-na (ci'i rgyus na) for what reason, why, wherefore Stg.; dé-bas-na (de bas na) hence, thus, so then, accordingly, very frq; kúr-bas-na (khur bas na) because they carried Glr.; also added to the termination of the termin.: ji-ltar-na (ji ltar na) frq.; γćig-tu-na (gcig tu na), gnyis-su-na (gnyis su na), in the first place, firstly etc. Dzl.; slád-du-na (slad du na) Dzl.; rgya-gár skád-du-na (rgya gar skad du na) Thgy. — 4. incorr. for nas (nas), col. frq.; its being used for the termin. is very questionable, and the rare instances of this use in books may be regarded as errors in writing (e.g. Dzl. 22, 17 naṅ-na soṅ (nang na song) inst. of naṅ-du (nang du)), whereas the con-trary, du (du) for na (na), occurs frq., and is to be considered as sanctioned. III. conj. and, Bal (?) — IV. v. ná-ka (na ka), ná-ba (na ba).
Sch. 1. being ill (?). 2. the claws of a sea-monster (?).
Lt. = Hindi, for नागकेसर Mesua ferrea.
W. mock-suns and similar phenomena, v. na-bún (na bun).
(Cs.: 'for na-rnyiṅ' (na rnying)) the last year; gen. adv. last year; ná-niṅ-gi (na ning gi) adj. of last year or last year's (crop).

1. to be ill, sick; inf. also the state of being ill, illness, sickness, ná-ba gsó-ba (na ba gso ba) to cure it Lt., though nad (nad) is more in use; partic.: a sick person, patient, ná-ba daṅ ₒći-ba (na ba dang 'chi ba) disease and death; skye rga na ₒći (skye rga na 'chi) v. skyé-ba (skye ba) I., rgás-pa daṅ ná-ba (rgas pa dang na ba) old and sick people; mi-ná-ba gnás-pa (mi na ba gnas pa) to remain in health S.g.; ná-ba-pa (na ba pa), ná-ba-ma (na ba ma) Cs. a sick person, an invalid (male and female); ná-mo (na mo) a female patient Mil.; ná-ba-mk'an (na ba mkhan) a sickly person, an invalid Cs.; ná-ba-ćan (na ba can) sickly, na-ba-méd (na ba med) healthy Cs.; na-tóg (na thog) after falling ill Sch. — 2. of the separate parts of the body: to ache, rná-ba (rna ba) (not -baï (-ba'i)) ná-ba (na ba) pain in the ear, earache; lus tams-ćád na (lus thams cad na) (my) whole body aches Dom.; so ná-na (so na na) having the tooth-ache; nán-na na (mnan na na) it aches, when pressed (with the fingers) S.g.; klád-pa ná-ba-la (klad pa na ba la) (good) for the headache, for diseases of the brain; na-ₒpréṅ (na 'phreng) complication of diseases or fits Sch.; na-(ba daṅ)zúg(-rṅu) (na ba dang zug rngu), na-tsá (na tsha) disease and pain
fog, thick mist, tibs (thibs), kyims ('khyims) comes on; byin-rlabs-kyi (byin rlabs kyi) prob. a cloud, a flood, of blessing Mil.
